data.title: Natural Gas Rises on Bullish Demand Outlook and Warming Forecasts
data.description:
Natural gas futures rose early on Wednesday after the Energy Information Administration issued a bullish outlook for the fuel a day earlier while long-term forecasts see heat on the way for big northern markets.

Gas for June delivery was last seen up US$0.14 to US$3.60 per million British thermal units.

The rise comes and the EIA's Short-Term Energy Outlook released on Tuesday raised its forecast for second-quarter gas prices to US$4,35 per mmBtu from US$4.18 on strong export demand and a solid domestic call on supply that it expects to continue.

"We now forecast that there will be more demand for natural gas domestically and for U.S. exports of natural gas over the next two years than we had initially forecast.," the agency wrote.

Long-term forecasts from the National Weather Service see hotter than seasonal weather for most states east of the Rocky Mountains over its six to 10 day outlook, with temperatures in the upper Midwest much warmer than normal.

data.title: UBS Adjusts Price Target on Datadog to $125 From $120, Maintains Buy Rating
data.description: Datadog Inc has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $139.76, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Wall Street gains ahead of Federal Reserve decision, hopes of US-China deal support sentiment
data.description:
US stocks inched higher on Wednesday, May 5, as investors awaited the U.S. Federal Reserve's monetary policy announcement due later in the session.

At 9.45 am ET or 7.15 pm IST, the Dow Jones rose by 200 points, or 0.5 percent, while the S&P 500 and he tech-heavy Nasdaq index edged up 0.3 percent each.

Investors are closely watching for the Federal Reserve’s rate decision at 2 pm ET, with CME's FedWatch tool suggesting a 97 percent chance that rates will remain unchanged.

Track the US Federal Reserve updates here

Despite a disappointing GDP print and concerns that new U.S. tariffs could reignite inflation, analysts widely expect the Federal Reserve to hold rates steady at 4.25 to 4.5 percent. The Fed appears to be buying time amid unresolved uncertainty around trade policy, inflation trajectories, and labor market resilience.

“If traders wish to believe that the Fed will come to the rescue of the world tomorrow and assuage the recent rise in policy uncertainty and political uncertainty with a signal of overt ‘dovishness’, they should think again,” said a Macquarie analyst.

Markets also gained after reports that U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and trade official Jamieson Greer would meet Chinese officials in Switzerland this week, signaling possible progress on trade talks after last month’s tariff turmoil. “We’ve got to deescalate before we move forward,” Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in an interview.

This would mark the first major trade discussion between the two trading partners, after U.S. President Donald Trump raised the tariff on imports from China to 145 percent in April.

Disney shares jumped over 6 percent after it beat earnings and revenue forecasts and reported unexpected growth in streaming subscribers. On the other hand, Uber shares dropped nearly 4 percent following weaker-than-expected revenue.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: ImmuneWalk Therapeutics Announces $7 Million Seed Financing and Upcoming Presentations of Positive Clinical Results at Upcoming Medical and Industry Conferences
data.description:
• Completed seed financing round of $7 million from an institutional investor and ImmuneWalk’s management team
  • Includes oral presentation of positive safety, pharmacokinetic, and pharmacodynamic data from immunology and inflammation (I&I) candidate IW-601’s Phase 1 single ascending dose study

NEW YORK and PETACH TIKVAH, Israel, May 07, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — ImmuneWalk Therapeutics, a clinical stage company focused on modulating immune and inflammatory disease through monocyte inhibition, today announced the completion of a $7 million seed financing from an institutional investor with participation from the ImmuneWalk management team. The company also announced it will be presenting positive data from the IW-601 first-in-human clinical trial at the EULAR 2025 Annual Meeting and BIO International Convention in June.

EULAR 2025 Congress

  • Format: Oral presentation
  • Date & Time: June 11, 2025, at 17:15-17:25 (UTC+01:00)
  • Clinical Abstract Sessions: Across Diseases
  • Subject: IW-601, a first-in-class monoclonal antibody targeting myeloid cells is safe and well tolerated. Pharmacodynamic assessments provide mechanistic validation; Results from IW-601-001 a first-in-human Phase 1 Single Ascending Doses study
  • Location: Fira de Barcelona, Barcelona, Spain, Hall A3

2025 BIO International Convention (Boston, MA)

  • Format: Oral presentation
  • Dates: June 16-19, 2025
  • Location: Boston Convention & Exhibition Center, Boston, MA

“IW-601’s unique mechanism of action, which inhibits monocytes and neutrophils’ ability to fuel inflammation, has the potential to address key unmet needs in various immune and inflammatory diseases and we are excited to share the initial single ascending dose data from its first-in-human trial at EULAR and BIO,” said Dror Harats, M.D., Chief Executive Officer of ImmuneWalk. “I’d like to thank our seed investors for their visionary support and continued commitment, helping us accelerate the development of IW-601 towards additional key value inflection points beginning later in 2025.”

About IW-601

IW-601 is a monoclonal antibody designed to target MOSPD2, an adhesion checkpoint protein found on monocytes and neutrophils. By inhibiting MOSPD2, IW-601 prevents these immune cells from migrating into inflammatory tissues, effectively reducing immune-driven inflammation without depleting blood cells. This novel mechanism distinguishes IW-601 from traditional therapies, offering a targeted approach to inflammatory diseases. Preclinical studies have shown strong activity in ex-vivo patient samples and in-vivo models of multiple sclerosis, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, MASH, and uveitis. For additional mechanistic information see our recent peer-reviewed publication (link).

About ImmuneWalk Therapeutics

​ImmuneWalk Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company dedicated to developing novel therapies for immune-inflammatory diseases. Central to their approach is the discovery of a new adhesion checkpoint that regulates the migration of specific immune cells into inflamed tissues. This innovative mechanism offers a differentiated approach compared to existing anti-inflammatory agents. Their lead candidate, IW-601, is an engineered monoclonal antibody that has demonstrated disease-modifying activity in preclinical models of multiple sclerosis, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, and metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis. The company is currently conducting the POINTGUARD Phase 1 clinical trial, with the Single Ascending Dose stage successfully completed and multiple catalysts anticipated in 2025-2026. For more information, visit www.immunewalk.com

About EULAR

The European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology, EULAR, is the organization which represents the patient, health professional and scientific societies of rheumatology of all the European nations. EULAR endeavors to stimulate, promote, and support the research, prevention, treatment and rehabilitation of rheumatic diseases.

About BIO

BIO is the world’s largest advocacy association representing biotechnology companies, academic and research institutions, state biotechnology centers and related organizations across the United States and in more than 30 other nations.

Investor and Media InquiriesSam Backenroth

info@immunewalk.com

data.title: Hain Celestial eyes its worst day on record after annual sales forecast cut; CEO steps down
data.description:
** Shares of organic and natural products company Hain Celestial fall as much as 53% to a more than three-decade low of $1.3

** Stock set for its worst day on record, if losses hold

** Co now expects annual organic net sales growth to be down about 5% to 6%, compared to previous forecast of down 2% to 4%, on slower-than-anticipated volume recovery, and softening and volatile macroeconomic environment

** Co misses Q3 revenue, profit estimates, according to data compiled by LSEG; cites worse-than-expected performance in North America

** Separately, co announced CEO Wendy Davidson is stepping down, effective today

** Co appoints Alison Lewis, a Hain executive, as interim CEO

** Hain also announces it is conducting a comprehensive review of the company’s portfolio with assistance of Goldman Sachs & Co, to consider strategic options to enhance value

** Stock has fallen ~55% YTD, as of last close

data.title: Johnson Controls rises after lifting 2025 profit forecast
data.description:
** Shares of liquid cooling systems maker Johnson Controls International rise 2% to $90

** Co raises 2025 profit forecast after beating Q2 expectations, helped by sustained demand from data centers for building and industrial equipment

** JCI expects 2025 adj profit of $3.60/share, the top end of previous forecast range of $3.50 to $3.60

** Co posts Q2 revenue of $5.68 bln, compared with analysts' avg estimates of $5.64 bln - data compiled by LSEG

** Excluding items, co posts profit of 82 cents/share, above estimates of 79 cents/share

** Thirteen of 23 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, 10 "hold"; their median PT is $90 - LSEG

** Including session moves, shares are up 14.4% YTD

data.title: Novo Nordisk Drops Gender Representation Goals in US
data.description:
Novo Nordisk said Wednesday its US operations will no longer take part in a global initiative on gender representation because of "evolving legal requirements."

The company said that worldwide, it continues to work to achieve representation of at least 45% for each gender by the end of this year.

"We remain committed to non-discrimination and innovation through diverse perspectives, always in compliance with local regulations," the Danish drugmaker said in its Q1 earnings release.

data.title: Uber Stock Tumbles on First-Quarter Earnings. Here's Why.
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Uber Technologies stock tumbled in premarket trading Wednesday after the ride-hailing platform posted mixed first-quarter results.

Earnings of 83 cents a share topped the 51 cents analysts had been expecting, according to FactSet. Revenue rose 14% from the prior year to $11.5 billion, but narrowly missed the $11.6 billion Wall Street had forecast.

Gross bookings rose 14% to $42.8 billion in the quarter. However, the figure came in below analysts' expectations of $43.1 billion.

Trips, or the number of completed rides and delivery orders in a given period, increased 18% to 3 billion. Uber attributed this change, in part, to growth in the number of monthly active users on the platform.

The company posted first-quarter adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization of $1.86 billion. At the time of its last earnings report in February, Uber said it expected Ebitda in the range of $1.79 billion to $1.89 billion.

The ride-hailing service guided for gross bookings in the second quarter of between $45.75 billion and $47.25 billion and adjusted Ebitda of $2.02 billion to $2.12 billion.

Uber stock fell 3.6% to $82.75 on Wednesday. Futures tracking the benchmark S&P 500 were up 0.6%.

Shares of Uber's largest competitor, Lyft, pared earlier losses to rise 1.6%.

One day before the report, Uber said it had acquired an 85% controlling stake in Trendyol GO, an online meal and grocery delivery service based in Turkey. The roughly $700 million deal comes as Uber seeks to expand its footprint in the food delivery market.

The company also continues to invest heavily in autonomous vehicles. Uber formally entered the driverless market in 2016 with the launch of its first test vehicle in Pittsburgh. The car was staffed with a human driver who would take the wheel if needed, Uber said.

Efforts to adopt self-driving technology have intensified over the past year. CEO Dara Khosrowshahi noted that the company made five new announcements related to AVs "in just the last week."

"Supported by the consistent strength of our core business, we continue to build towards the future," Khosrowshahi said in a statement. The company has partnered with a host of AV companies including Waymo, the Alphabet-owned robotaxi service.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Mali suspends political activities to preserve public order, statement says
data.description:
Mali has suspended political activities across the West African nation to preserve public order, according to a statement read on state TV on Wednesday.

Mali's political parties were expected to protest on Friday against the military junta in power. They are demanding a return to constitutional order and a presidential election.

However, the junta said in its statement that activities of political parties and other political organizations had been suspended until further notice.

data.title: Polish central bank cuts rates by 50 bps in first easing since 2023
data.description:
The National Bank of Poland cut interest rates for the first time since October 2023 on Wednesday, slashing the cost of credit by 50 basis points in a move that analysts had expected and reflects falling inflation.

The key interest rate fell to 5.25%, the central bank said, and attention will now turn to a press release published after the decision, which could indicate the path for further easing.

"The arguments for the cut include a clear slowdown in inflation, decreasing concerns about the increase in energy prices for households in the fall, a slowdown in wage growth and weaker than expected economic results in the first quarter of 2025," PKO BP analysts said in a note ahead of the decision.

A flash reading last week showed Poland's consumer price index in April falling to 4.2% from 4.9% in March. The NBP inflation target is 2.5%, with a tolerance band of plus or minus one percentage point.

The market had been speculating for months that the central bank would finally cut the cost of credit, and expectations of this increased in April, when Adam Glapinski, governor of the National Bank of Poland, made a "dovish turn" and said that rates could be cut as early as May.

Also some members of the Monetary Policy Council had, even earlier than Glapinski, signalled that rates could fall, and some of them indicated May as the right moment to make such a decision.

Currently, the market is waiting for further decisions easing monetary policy, and some analysts believe that rates may fall again in June. Others believe the MPC may do so in July, when a new inflation projection will be published.

The market will be perusing the next set of data closely, especially wage growth, the pace of which, although it has fallen from a double-digit level, still remains high. The government's loose fiscal policy is also raising concerns.

More signals regarding future monetary policy may be provided at the conference of NBP president Glapiński scheduled for Thursday.

data.title: WEC Energy Group Is Maintained at Overweight by Wells Fargo
ICE Canola on the Rise
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange continued to push higher on Wednesday morning, building on gains made in the overnight session.

The July contract was testing resistance at the psychological level of C$700 per tonne. It also remained well above its major moving averages, which underpin values.

Gains in the Chicago soy complex and European rapeseed spilled over into canola. However, declines in Malaysian palm oil limited the increases. Crude oil was virtually unchanged, offering little direction to the vegetable oils.

Statistics Canada is set to release its report on grain stocks as of March 31 on Thursday. The report could indicate how much canola there is in the country, as the belief in the trade is StatCan has underestimated the fall harvest by up to 1.5 million tonnes.

The Canadian dollar dipped on Wednesday morning, with the loonie at 72.49 U.S. cents, compared with Tuesday's close of 72.55.

Approximately 10,050 contracts were traded by 9:43 ET and prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne were:

 
           Price      Change 
Jul       697.70     up 4.00 
Nov       662.00     up 6.10 
Jan       669.80     up 5.00 
Mar       677.50     up 5.00

data.title: Nokian Tyres plc: Decisions of the Board of Directors' organizing meeting
data.description:
Nokian Tyres

Nokian Tyres plc Stock Exchange Release May 7, 2025, at 4:50 p.m. EEST

The Board of Directors of Nokian Tyres held its organisational meeting today.

The Board of Directors elected Jouko Pölönen as the Chair and Elisa Markula and Antti Mäkinen as members of the Audit Committee.

The Board of Directors elected Elina Björklund as the Chair and Susanne Hahn and Jukka Hienonen and as members of the People and Sustainability Committee.

The Board of Directors elected Christopher Ostrander as the Chair and Markus Korsten and Jukka Hienonen as members of the Investment Committee.

Further information: 

Elisa Erkkilä, General Counsel, SVP Legal

Tel. +358 10 401 7050

Investor relations: ir@nokiantyres.com 

Media enquiries: media@nokiantyres.com 

More information on the Board's Committees and members can be found on Nokian Tyres website: company.nokiantyres.com/investors/corporate-governance/board-of-directors/

Nokian Tyres' purpose is to make the world safer by reinventing tires, and how they are made, over and over again - a safer place to drive, work and live now and for generations to come. Inspired by our northern heritage, we develop and manufacture premium tires for passenger cars, trucks and heavy machinery with sustainability at the heart of all our operations. Our Vianor chain provides tire and car services. We are some 3,800 people with net sales of EUR 1.3 billion in 2024, and together we lead the journey to smarter driving for people and for businesses. Nokian Tyres is listed on Nasdaq Helsinki. Further information: company.nokiantyres.com,www.nokiantyres.com.

data.title: Asset Entities to Merge With Strive to Launch Bitcoin Treasury Firm; Shares Rise Sharply
data.description:
Asset Entities shares were gaining more than 190% Wednesday after the company said it will merge with Strive Asset Management to form a public company.

The combined company will operate as a public Bitcoin treasury under the Strive name and remain listed on Nasdaq, Asset Entities said.

The company said it plans to use a Bitcoin-for-stock exchange process to accumulate Bitcoin, expected to qualify as tax-free under Section 351 of the US tax code.

The multimedia marketing company said the transaction will be structured as a reverse merger and is expected to give the company immediate access to an existing shelf registration, which it plans to expand to $1 billion following closing.

Asset Entities said Strive CEO Matt Cole will lead the combined company.

After the deal closes, Strive Enterprises will hold about 94.2% of the public company, with Asset Entities' legacy shareholders holding the rest, excluding effects from the planned Bitcoin exchange and future financings.

data.title: CrowdStrike to layoff about 5% of its workforce; shares down
data.description:
** Shares of cybersecurity company CrowdStrike slip 4.7% to $422.38 in premarket trading

** CRWD to layoff about 500 employees, roughly 5% of its workforce, and will incur about $36 mln to $53 mln in charges related to layoffs, of which about $7 mln will be recognized in Q1 ended April 30

** Co expects 2026 Q1 results to be in-line or above forecast; reaffirms guidance for FY 2026

** CRWD will release Q1 results on June 3 after markets close

** As of last close, CRWD up 29.5% YTD vs S&P 500 information technology index's decline of nearly 10%

data.title: Infineon Expected to Keep Full-Year Guidance Amid Tariff UncertaintyEarnings Preview
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Infineon Technologies is scheduled to report results for its fiscal second quarter on Thursday. Here is what you need to know.

REVENUE FORECAST: The German chip maker is expected to post revenue of 3.61 billion euros ($4.10 billion) for the January-through-March quarter, according to consensus estimates compiled by Vara Research and based on estimates by 19 analysts. For the same quarter of fiscal 2024, Infineon reported revenue of 3.63 billion euros.

EARNINGS FORECAST: Net profit is expected to fall to 318 million euros, according to the consensus. Infineon made a net profit of 394 million euros in the year-earlier period. Infineon's segment result--a key profitability metric--is forecast by the consensus at 572 million euros, down from 707 million euros a year before.

Shares in Infineon fell around 17% over the past year. During the quarter to March, they fell 3.2%.

WHAT TO WATCH

  • GUIDANCE: Infineon is likely to stand by existing forecasts for the year to September, with the caveat of tariff uncertainty, analysts at Morgan Stanley said in a research note. The company guided in February for fiscal 2025 revenue to be flat or slightly up, an adjusted gross margin around 40% and a segment result margin in the mid-to-high-teens percentage range. If Infineon updates its currency assumptions, the move could result in a 200 million-euro reduction in its segment result, JPMorgan analysts estimated. A confirmation of guidance despite the currency headwinds will probably be welcomed by investors, but would require new revenue not currently factored into consensus expectations, the JPM analysts said.
  • TARIFFS: It seems unlikely that Infineon investors will get any steer on what impact tariffs might have given the lack of clarity around their implementation, Morgan Stanley analysts said. Peers that have reported so far such as STMicroelectronics, Texas Instruments, Renesas Electronics and NXP Semiconductors haven't been able to give guidance on what level of revenue could be at risk, Morgan Stanley analysts said. Resilient electric vehicle sales in China and Europe could provide some buffer, UBS analyst Francois-Xavier Bouvignies said.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Aarti Drugs Consolidated Net Profit Jumps in Fiscal Q4
data.description:
Aarti Drugs attributable consolidated net profit jumped to 614.8 million Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter from 361.4 million rupees a year ago, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

Earnings per share during the quarter ended March 31 climbed to 6.74 rupees from 3.93 rupees.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q4 rose to 6.23 billion rupees from 5.60 billion rupees in the corresponding quarter of the previous fiscal year.

data.title: Mexico central bank still has room to lower benchmark rate, Heath says
data.description:
There is still room for Mexico's central bank to lower its benchmark interest rate, deputy governor Jonathan Heath said in a podcast with lender Banorte published on Wednesday.

Last month, the Bank of Mexico cut its interest rate 50 basis points to 9% in a unanimous decision, while its board also warned of uncertainty linked to trade tensions worldwide.

Heath added that it was highly likely that the monetary authority would continue lowering the rate, while maintaining caution amid an uncertain economic scenario.

Speaking about the current economic uncertainty linked to tariffs imposed by the U.S. government, Heath noted that "in the face of all these risks, we are still underpinning a restrictive monetary stance, but less and less restrictive than we had previously anticipated."

He added that all scenarios considered by the central bank expected a slowdown in Mexico's economy, as well as in the United States.

data.title: U.S. senators announce bipartisan push to change how Fed watchdog selected
data.description:
Two senators announced legislation on Tuesday that would change how the Federal Reserve’s in-house watchdog is selected.

In a press release Senator Rick Scott, a Republican, and Senator Elizabeth Warren, a Democrat, said their bill would require the Federal Reserve’s inspector general, or IG, to be named by the president and confirmed by the Senate, breaking from the current practice where the watchdog is selected to serve by the chair of the Federal Reserve.

The senators say the fact the IG is selected by the same person whose agency he oversees “poses serious conflicts of interest,” according to a press release. The legislation comes as the Fed’s most recent IG, Mark Bialek, resigned earlier this year, in a development first reported by Politico.

The senators’ press release said Bialek “failed to hold Federal Reserve employees accountable amid ethics concerns, regulatory failures, and bank collapses.” The Fed’s IG did not immediately respond to a request for comment. A Fed spokesperson acknowledged getting a letter from the senators over the IG selection process and said the central bank plans to respond.

The Fed’s Office of the Inspector General is responsible for overseeing the Fed, as well as the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. It conducts audits, investigations and makes recommendations for the two agencies.

The Fed’s IG has faced criticism for going too soft on the Fed and some in the Senate have been calling for an overhaul to make the IG’s selection process more like others who do similar work in the government. The IG’s work has been in the public eye of late as it investigated a trading controversy that affected several central bank officials.

data.title: Fed Press Conference Might Not Add Much NewMarket Talk
data.description:
0946 ET - Jefferies economist Thomas Simons extends his sympathies to hard-working members of the Federal Reserve press corps: "It is difficult to imagine how they can come up with questions that Powell can and will provide a straight answer to regarding the outlook for policy or the economy," Simons writes. Simons expects Fed Chair Jerome Powell to play it close to the vest at this afternoon's press conference, maintaining a similar tone from his last presser in March. Tariff-induced inflation remains a concern, but beyond that, "we see a balanced view on the dual mandate that does not express limitation in the Fed's capacity to provide accommodation if the growth and labor market outlook were to weaken materially," Simons writes. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

0938 ET - The euro could benefit from the de-dollarization in foreign exchange reserves but there's more work to do to increase the attractiveness of euro-denominated assets, ING analysts say in a note. "Should central banks' appetite for euros return to what it was in the early 2000s, euro-denominated FX reserves could increase by as much as around 450 billion euros." De-dollarization is helped by the net issuance of Treasurys being mainly absorbed by domestic private investors with key Asian investors exiting the market. In order for Europe to attract these flows, it needs to provide a high quality and liquid pool of assets to compete with Treasurys.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0852 ET - The Czech koruna stays higher against the euro after the Czech National Bank cut its benchmark two-week repo rate by 25 basis points to 3.50%, as expected by analysts. The central bank also lowered its discount rate and the Lombard rate by 25bp to 2.50% and 4.50% respectively. The move comes after inflation data for April came in below expectations. "We think that headline inflation is likely to rise over the rest of the year to the top half of the central bank's 1-3% target range, providing limited grounds for further rate cuts," Capital Economics economist Nicholas Farr says in a note. The euro falls 0.3% to 24.8980 koruna, compared to 24.9199 before the decision. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0841 ET - Bond markets look upbeat ahead of the Fed's decision later today, and word that U.S. and Chinese officials will meet for trade talks. The Fed is expected to keep rates on hold. Investors will tune in for Chair Powell's post-meeting remarks, as he is likely to be asked about how tariffs are affecting inflation and economic growth. Risk-taking returns after two-day retreat, with Treasury yields rising. The 10-year is at 4.320% and the two-year at 3.814%.(paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0731 ET - Sterling could weaken if the Bank of England's policy decision on Thursday hints at ramping up interest rate cuts, TD Securities analysts say in a note. The BOE is likely to vote 8-1 to cut rates by 25 basis points, but with policymaker Swati Dhingra voting in favor of a 50 bp cut, they say. The BOE is also likely to downgrade its economic growth forecasts and drop the word "gradual" from its guidance for further rate cuts amid heightened trade uncertainty. "Inflation has come in below market expectations and monetary policy report projections, giving members confidence in continuing on a cutting path." Sterling is last down 0.1% at $1.3351. The euro rises 0.1% to 0.8517. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0729 ET - Gold futures pull back after rallying in the two prior sessions. Futures are down 0.8% at $3,394.20 a troy ounce, but remain up 1.8% on week. A downward correction or at least a period of consolidation is reasonable given the size of Monday and Tuesday's gains, Trade Nation's David Morrison writes. The retreat is likely on news that the U.S. and China are preparing for trade talks this weekend, Morrison says. While these are preliminary discussions, traders took this apparent softening in hardline attitudes as a reason to offload some safe-haven positions and increase risk exposure, Morrison says. That said, compared to previous gold rallies, European retail investors have been absent, gold-miners equities have failed to catch up and silver prices have largely decoupled--making the short-term outlook for gold unclear. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0730 ET - Despite Friedrich Merz's failure to be elected German chancellor in the first round of parliamentary voting, his government will likely be able to implement its agenda, Berenberg's Holger Schmieding says in a note. "Merz has a proven ability to recover from setbacks. For example, it had taken him three attempts to become head of his CDU party--but he still made it in the end," Schmieding says. Still, the delay in the Bundestag means he looks weaker than expected and may not rely on full support from the two parties of his coalition. "To some extent, it may impair Merz's domestic and international authority at least initially," he warns. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0659 ET - German manufacturing orders easily beat consensus in March, though trade uncertainty will weigh on activity ahead, Pantheon Macroeconomics' Claus Vistesen says in an note. Frontloading by U.S. importers ahead of tariffs likely contributed, though the scale of the impact is difficult to quantify, he says. Pharmaceutical orders jumped 17%, partly reflecting efforts by U.S. imports to get ahead of likely tariffs in the sector. But trade concerns will continue to weigh on investment, even as exports perform strongly and consumption growth slows only marginally. There is an upside risk, however, should firms look through trade concerns on greater domestic political clarity under the new government led by Chancellor Merz, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0654 ET - The cost of insuring euro high-yield credit against default falls to its lowest since April 2, just before U.S. President Trump's announcement of widespread tariffs caused market turmoil. Optimism surrounding U.S.-China trade talks has helped boost sentiment, with U.S. stock futures pointing to a higher open. Markets hope that trade talks between Washington and Beijing this week can result in "some de-escalation in the tit-for-tat tariffs between the two major economies," says AJ Bell's Russ Mould in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index, which tracks euro junk bond credit default swaps, falls 2 basis points to 330 bps, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. The iTraxx Europe Main index of euro investment-grade CDS stays at 64 bps. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0650 ET - The dollar won't necessarily appreciate if the Federal Reserve sounds cautious about the potential for cutting interest rates in the coming months at Wednesday's meeting, MUFG Bank analyst Derek Halpenny says in a note. Since the start of April, the euro has strengthened even though the two-year EU-U.S. yield spread has moved in favor of the U.S., he says. That means cautious signals about rate cuts won't necessarily lift the dollar. Uncertainty over policy and strong expectations that the U.S. economy will weaken mean the dollar will likely either stay weak or weaken further, Halpenny says. The Fed announces a decision is at 1800 GMT and is expected to leave rates unchanged. The DXY dollar index rises 0.2% to 99.404. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0635 ET - Increased wages aren't spurring eurozone shoppers to the stores, ING economist Bert Colijn notes after retail volumes fell back across the 20-member currency union. The 0.1% decrease in March reverses some of the gains made in February and leaves sales essentially flat since last September, before President Trump's election coincided with a sheering off of confidence among European consumers. Wage growth is now outstripping price inflation in the eurozone, boosting incomes and purchasing power, but consumer aren't yet using those gains to spend more at the store, Colijn says. "For the months ahead, we expect that the global economic environment will continue to spook consumers, but modest growth in spending could still be in the making," he tells clients in a note.(joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0617 ET - More of the same is expected on Wednesday from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, who is likely to stress policymakers need to see more data and aren't in a rush to cut interest rates, says Aptus Capital Advisors' John Luke Tyner. Powell won't be "ultra-proactive on future cuts" given inflation is still above target and the economy appears okay, the head of fixed income and portfolio manager says. Tyner is keen to hear comments on tariffs, particularly their potential impact on prices. Powell will likely stress Fed independence following recent comments from Trump's administration, he says. The 10-year Treasury yield rises 0.5 basis points to 4.312%, according to Tradeweb. The Fed's announcement is at 1800 GMT. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Street View: AMD to face China export snags, MI chips shows promise
data.description:
** Advanced Micro Devices on Tuesday forecast a $1.5 billion hit to revenue this year due to new U.S. curbs on chips, which require the company to obtain a license to ship advanced artificial-intelligence processors to China

** Shares of the chipmaker up 3.3%

AI GAMBIT

** JP Morgan ("neutral", PT: $120) sees AMD's datacenter GPU revenue facing significant challenges due to export restrictions, but anticipates strong growth driven by MI350 (AI accelerator GPU) series

** Jefferies ("hold", PT: $100) says AI growth estimates are falling due to the China ban, and despite strong client and server revenues, the outlook remains cautious without a clear AI growth story

** TD Cowen ("buy", PT: $115) sees early traction with Oracle and expectations of new customers as positive indicators for AMD's AI strategy

** HSBC ("reduce", PT: $75) believes eliminating MI308 AI GPU will boost co's gross margin in latter half of 2025 and is cautiously optimistic about MI400 AI server rack's potential next year

** "We expect AMD to continue to outperform overall non-AI data centre and client segment," says HSBC
